title: Nimbadesk SaaS product sell sheets: grade product screenshots + vectorize logo + InDesign data-merge one row per product tier into print PDFs + high-res JPEGs

source.reference: Marketing Collateral & Brand Assets (Legal / MVA Case Management)

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: "DELIVERABLE 2 — One-Page Service Overview: single/double-sided 8.5\"x11\" sell sheet"; "header with branding and compelling title"; a "\"company advantage\" section"; "core-services bullets"; "a prominent CTA"; "clean grids, profess[ional]" layout; overall deliverables "must feel clean, modern, trustworthy and polished, referencing the website's palette/typography." REASONABLY ENRICHED to be a runnable Adobe-only, tech_software_saas benchmark: the original brief's legal/MVA case-management firm is replaced with a fictional, IP-clean B2B SaaS company ("Nimbadesk"), and the single one-pager is scaled into a SMALL SET of per-product-tier sell sheets so the job is a genuine InDesign data-merge (one row per tier) rather than a single hand-set page. From-scratch layout is NOT in connector scope (document_render_layout / document_merge_data_layout only EXPORT/bind a GENUINE authored .indd carrying real <<fields>>). So the client is modeled as supplying the authored one-page sell-sheet template with real merge fields + image frames, a brand kit, the raw product screenshots, the logo PNG, and the product-tier CSV — a normal freelancer hand-off / production-designer revision scenario. The Adobe chain then does the doable heavy lifting the brief rewards: cleaning up the dull/tilted product screenshots, tinting them on-brand, vectorizing the logo for crisp print, recommending a body font, binding the tier copy via real InDesign data-merge, and exporting print PDFs + high-res JPEGs. No deliverable was invented beyond the sell sheet(s) the brief asks for.
one_line_ask: Clean up and on-brand-tint three raw product-UI screenshots, vectorize the Nimbadesk logo, then run the client's authored one-page sell-sheet InDesign template through a data-merge against a product-tier CSV to produce a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG per tier.
full_brief: Nimbadesk, a B2B SaaS company that sells a customer-support help-desk platform, needs its product sell sheets finished for print and for the sales team's leave-behind kit. Our production designer has already authored the layout: a one-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11" portrait) sell-sheet InDesign template (nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd) built on a clean modern grid, with a branded header, a "Why teams switch to Nimbadesk" company-advantage section, a core-features bullet block, a prominent "Book a demo" CTA, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<tier_name>>, <<tagline>>, <<price_line>>, <<feature_1>>, <<feature_2>>, <<feature_3>>, <<cta_line>>) plus a hero image frame for the product screenshot and a logo frame. This is a production/finishing job, NOT a fresh branding exercise. We will hand off: the authored .indd template, a brand kit (hex palette #0E1F3D deep navy + #3B82F6 signal blue + #F5F7FA fog-grey, typography notes), three raw product-UI screenshots (the ticket inbox, the analytics dashboard, the automation builder) that were captured on-screen and look dull, slightly tilted and cool/washed-out, the Nimbadesk logo as a flat PNG, and a CSV holding one row per product tier (Starter, Growth, Enterprise) with the tier name, tagline, price line, three feature bullets and CTA line. The job: (1) make each product screenshot look crisp and on-brand — straighten it, auto-tone to fix the washed-out capture, warm/neutralize the cool cast, lift the shadows so dark-UI detail reads, nudge brightness/contrast, and lay a subtle signal-blue monochromatic tint so the three shots feel like one brand family; consistently crop all three to the same 16:10 ratio the hero frame expects; (2) vectorize the flat logo PNG into a clean SVG so the wordmark stays razor-sharp at print size and on the large header; (3) recommend a professional, legible sans-serif body font that pairs with the brand's geometric feel (for the designer's reference note); (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the tier CSV so each tier's copy binds into its own page of the template with the matching prepped screenshot in the hero frame and the vector logo in the header; (5) export the merged multi-page document to a print-ready PDF and a high-resolution JPEG deliverable. Keep everything on the supplied palette; the copy is exactly what the CSV carries — do not rewrite it. Final files must be print-ready (~300 DPI, US Letter portrait).

inputs.name: nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a one-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11", portrait) B2B SaaS product sell sheet on a clean modern grid. Must contain REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields named <<tier_name>>, <<tagline>>, <<price_line>>, <<feature_1>>, <<feature_2>>, <<feature_3>>, <<cta_line>> arranged into a branded header, a 'Why teams switch to Nimbadesk' company-advantage block, a three-bullet core-features list, and a prominent 'Book a demo' CTA band; plus a hero image frame sized to a 16:10 ratio for the product screenshot and a logo frame in the header. Uses the brand palette (#0E1F3D navy, #3B82F6 signal blue, #F5F7FA fog-grey). Authored by hand in InDesign so the data-merge fields actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own authored production template (a normal freelancer finishing/revision hand-off). document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is a supplied input, not a chain output.

inputs.name: nimbadesk_brand_kit.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a concise one-page brand-kit reference PDF for a fictional B2B SaaS brand 'Nimbadesk': primary palette deep navy #0E1F3D, signal blue #3B82F6, fog-grey #F5F7FA; heading font is a geometric sans; body font TBD (to be recommended); logo clear-space and minimum-size rules; tone = clean, modern, trustworthy. Note the sell sheet must reference this palette/typography.
inputs.realism_notes: Stands in for the brief's 'referencing the website's palette/typography'; drives the on-brand tint colour and the font-recommend step. IP-clean fictional brand.

inputs.name: screenshot_ticket_inbox.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A helpdesk ticket-inbox screen for a fictional SaaS called 'Nimbadesk': a dark narrow left icon rail, a ticket-list column headed 'Active Tickets' with a few short conversation titles and times, and a main panel showing one open ticket with a short customer message and a reply box with a 'Send' button, plus small status chips 'Open' and 'Priority: Medium'. Render as a CLEAN full-bleed software UI SCREENSHOT — the app interface fills the ENTIRE frame with NO physical monitor, NO desk, NO bezel, NO device and NO room visible. Modern flat SaaS design. ALL text labels are short, real English words, clearly legible and correctly spelled — absolutely no gibberish or garbled text. Fictional product name only. NO real brand names or logos anywhere (no HP, Dell, Apple, etc.).
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (tilt, flat/cool, washed-out) so the straighten + tone + temperature + tint chain has real work; gemini-3-pro-image for crisp legible UI text.

inputs.name: screenshot_analytics_dashboard.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal screen capture of a SaaS 'analytics dashboard' web UI: KPI cards (tickets resolved, CSAT, first-response time), a line chart and a bar chart, a data table. Realistic dark-ish dashboard theme. Captured dull and underexposed with a cool cast and crushed shadows so chart detail is hard to read, slight tilt, faint moire from the screen. No real brand marks.
inputs.realism_notes: Underexposed dark-UI capture justifies the shadow-lift + brightness/contrast + tint steps; crushed shadows make the dark-portions adjust meaningful.

inputs.name: screenshot_automation_builder.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A workflow-automation builder screen for the fictional SaaS 'Nimbadesk': a canvas with three or four connected rounded step nodes labelled 'Trigger', 'Condition' and 'Send Email', a slim left toolbox of blocks, and a right settings panel with a few short labelled fields and a blue 'Save' button. Render as a CLEAN full-bleed software UI SCREENSHOT — the app interface fills the ENTIRE frame with NO physical monitor, NO desk, NO bezel, NO device and NO room visible. Modern flat SaaS design. ALL text labels are short, real English words, clearly legible and correctly spelled — absolutely no gibberish or garbled text. Fictional product name only. NO real brand names or logos anywhere (no HP, Dell, Apple, etc.).
inputs.realism_notes: Third hero capture; same imperfections so all three screenshots can be graded and tinted to one consistent on-brand family.

inputs.name: nimbadesk_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat modern SaaS wordmark logo reading 'Nimbadesk' in a clean geometric sans-serif beside a small simple rounded-square app icon, on a SOLID FLAT off-white background, crisp clean edges, centred, horizontal. Not on a monitor, not a mockup, never a checkerboard pattern.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible wordmark; flat high-contrast + transparent bg so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG. Fictional, IP-clean mark.

inputs.name: nimbadesk_tiers.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns tier_name,tagline,price_line,feature_1,feature_2,feature_3,cta_line holding exactly three rows for product tiers Starter, Growth, Enterprise. Each row: a short benefit tagline, a price line (e.g. '$19 / agent / mo'), three concise feature bullets appropriate to that tier (e.g. shared inbox / SLA rules / advanced analytics / SSO / dedicated CSM), and a CTA line ('Book a demo'). Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields <<tier_name>>,<<tagline>>,<<price_line>>,<<feature_1>>,<<feature_2>>,<<feature_3>>,<<cta_line>>.
inputs.realism_notes: One row per product tier drives the data-merge to produce one bound sell-sheet page per tier; headers exactly match the authored .indd merge fields so the [T] merge binds.

outputs.name: Nimbadesk_SellSheets_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready multi-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11", portrait) PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document — one page per product tier (Starter, Growth, Enterprise), tier copy bound from the CSV, prepped on-brand screenshot in each hero frame, vector logo in the header; ~300 DPI print quality.

outputs.name: Nimbadesk_SellSheets_highres.jpg
outputs.spec: High-resolution JPEG export of the same bound sell-sheet document (US Letter portrait, max quality) for the sales team's digital leave-behind and email use.

outputs.name: graded_screenshots_x3
outputs.spec: Three cleaned, straightened, on-brand-tinted product screenshots (ticket inbox / analytics dashboard / automation builder), consistently 16:10-cropped to match the template hero frame.

outputs.name: nimbadesk_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the Nimbadesk wordmark for razor-sharp scaling in the sell-sheet header and at large print size.

outputs.name: sellsheet_body_font_note.txt
outputs.spec: A short recipe note recording the recommended body font (from font_recommend) that pairs with the brand's geometric heading font, for the designer's reference.

connector_workflow.note: Level the ~2-degree tilt on the ticket-inbox screen capture.
Auto-tone the straightened inbox shot to fix the flat/washed-out capture.
Warm/neutralize the cool bluish cast so the UI whites read neutral.
Nudge brightness/contrast so the small UI label text stays crisp.
Lay a subtle signal-blue (#3B82F6) monochromatic tint so the screenshot reads on-brand (same tint reused for the other two).
Crop/resize to the 16:10 hero frame the template expects (same target applied to all three).
Run the analytics-dashboard capture through the same grade, starting with auto-tone (fixes underexposure).
Lift the crushed shadows so the dark-theme chart/table detail becomes readable.
Neutralize the cool cast to match the inbox shot's white balance.
Apply the same signal-blue tint so the dashboard matches the brand family.
Crop the dashboard to the matching 16:10 hero frame.
Auto-tone the automation-builder capture to the consistent look.
Neutralize the cool white balance on the automation-builder shot.
Apply the same signal-blue tint so all three screenshots read as one brand family.
Crop the automation-builder shot to the 16:10 hero frame so all three match.
Vectorize the flat logo PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp large-print and header scaling.
Recommend a legible sans-serif body font that pairs with the brand's geometric heading font, per the brand kit.
Emit the recommended body font as a short reference note for the designer.
Initialize an upload session for all prepped sell-sheet assets (three graded screenshots + vector logo).
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the hero frame and header logo.

InDesign data-merge: bind each tier row (Starter/Growth/Enterprise) into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>), producing one bound page per tier with the prepped screenshot in the hero frame and the vector logo in the header. [T] requires the user-authored template.
Export the merged multi-page InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI US-Letter PDF deliverable.
Export the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG deliverable for the digital leave-behind.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chaining throughout. The ticket-inbox screenshot runs a 6-link grade pipeline: screenshot_ticket_inbox.png -> auto_straighten(1) -> auto_tone(2) -> color_temperature(3) -> brightness_contrast(4) -> monochromatic_tint(5) -> crop(6), each step consuming only the prior step's output. The analytics-dashboard screenshot feeds its own grade->crop sub-chain (7-11, including the shadow-lift at 8), and the automation-builder screenshot feeds a third (12-15). The logo chains raw PNG->vectorize(16); the brand kit chains font_recommend(17)->recipe note(18). All prepped image/vector outputs (inbox/dash/auto _cropped_16x10, logo_svg) converge into the upload session(19)->finalize(20)->data-merge(21), which also consumes the authored nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd + tiers CSV. The merged multi-page .indd(21) is the single source for both exports — render PDF(22) and render JPEG(23). Every deliverable emerges only from the assembled chain; nothing after the raw input loads originates outside it.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon: 23 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools plus one local step — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running three separate product screenshots through a consistent multi-step clean-up-and-tint pipeline (with a dark-UI shadow-lift branch on the dashboard), vectorizing a logo, sourcing a font recommendation, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge that binds one row per product tier into its own page, plus dual print/JPEG export. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / generative-expand / object-removal / text-to-image / from-scratch layout / compositing / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied authored input; the monochromatic tint and crop are whole-image operations; copy is honored literally from the CSV per the brief.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capabilities used. No image_crop_and_resize / generative fill / reframe-crop; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (nimbadesk_logo.png is a supplied input and is only vectorized, not generated); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal / inpainting of occluded pixels; no bespoke font design (font_recommend only selects an existing font); no unreachable external accounts. image_fill_area is not used at all.
reverify.notes: Corrected distinct_adobe_tools from 12 to 13 (the connector_workflow uses 13 distinct adobe-server tools: image_auto_straighten, image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_color_temperature, image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast, image_apply_monochromatic_tint, image_crop_and_resize, image_adjust_dark_portions, image_vectorize, font_recommend, asset_initialize_file_upload, asset_finalize_file_upload, document_merge_data_layout, document_render_layout; local_write_recipe_note is server=local and excluded). tool_call_count=23 matches the 23 workflow steps. Slot-match confirmed against L08.json: category datamerge_print / operation O5 / family Layout & Data / output document / workflow_nature create / vertical tech_software_saas. Grounded in the real Upwork candidate 'Marketing Collateral & Brand Assets (Legal / MVA Case Management)' whose DELIVERABLE 2 is a one-page 8.5x11 sell sheet with header/branding, company-advantage section, core-services bullets and a prominent CTA; grounding_note quotes it verbatim; fictional IP-clean brand 'Nimbadesk' replaces the real firm. document_merge_data_layout (n21) is correctly tagged [T] and binds against a supplied USER-AUTHORED nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd (kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset') carrying real <<fields>>; CSV headers exactly match those merge fields and the spec calls out that a PDF-converted .indd would not bind. document_render_layout (n22/n23) only EXPORTS that genuine authored/merged .indd -> PDF/JPEG; it does not compose a layout. image_apply_monochromatic_tint (n5,n10,n14), image_adjust_dark_portions (n8), image_adjust_color_temperature, image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast, image_auto_straighten, image_apply_auto_tone and image_crop_and_resize are all whole-image raster edits on supplied screenshots (no masked-DOF or compositing claim). image_vectorize (n16) turns a supplied flat PNG into an SVG. font_recommend (n17) inspects the brand kit; local_write_recipe_note (n18) emits the reference note. asset_initialize/finalize_file_upload move prepped assets into CC storage for the merge. All Adobe steps server=='adobe'; the single [L] step is server=='local'. No Express-widget tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color) and no Canva tools present.
